title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 2026
summary: Australia's dual-track Travel Rule enforcement and stablecoin PSP licensing, South Korea's Q4 custody framework comment deadline, and Indonesia's live Q3 OJK custody standards create a 90-day institutional compliance sprint across 200+ banks, exchanges, and PSPs from Sydney to Seoul. Philippines BSP active audits, Thailand's Q3 licensing expansion, and India's securities classification framework combine to open $180M+ addressable market through September.

title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: MiCA's July 2026 operational deadline triggers a synchronized compliance sprint across 27 EU member states as France's PSAN-to-MiCA transition goes live, AMLA launches with direct CASP supervision authority, and UK FCA sets firm authorization deadlines—creating a 90-day window where 400+ crypto firms, banks, and payment processors from Dublin to Dubai must operationalize travel rule infrastructure, stablecoin reserve monitoring, and market abuse detection systems.
